heading: ## Template manifest
reason: Landed in v063 (template-manifest-additional-files proposal accept); revised at v105 (Mermaid-first) and v136 (Mermaid the default; diagram-rendering machinery removed). The section's load-bearing rule is the manifest-aware doctor-static presence enumeration ('Heading-coverage and doctor-static rewiring'): under a v2 template the spec_files manifest is the source of truth for which files MUST be present. The mapped test seeds a v2 manifest declaring spec.md/contracts.md/constraints.md, leaves only spec.md on disk, and asserts doctor names the two missing manifest-declared files (sorted). The complementary heading_coverage.py manifest rewiring ships from the livespec-dev-tooling sibling; no live drift meanwhile since every fleet consumer runs a v1 template whose implicit manifest equals the check's built-in file tuple.

heading: ## Workflow planes and the Planning Lane

Descriptive architectural frame with no standalone CLI behavior; the test ID is populated if/when a behavioral clause attaches. Companion to the sibling '## Contract + reference implementations architecture' entry, which is likewise TODO.

heading: ## Orchestrator CLI contract — the three named CLIs
reason: Landed in v103 (contract-and-reference-implementations-phase-1 proposal accept). The Phase-2 impl follow-up wired the three orchestrator-side CLIs (spec-reader, gap-capture, drift-capture) through .livespec.jsonc. The mapped test asserts a complete orchestrator section round-trips its name plus all three argv-form CLIs through livespec_config validation; the sibling test_validate_livespec_config_orchestrator_missing_cli_is_rejected asserts a partial section is rejected, since the three CLIs are the whole cross-boundary contract.

heading: ## Spec-side CLI contract
reason: Landed in v103 (contract-and-reference-implementations-phase-1 proposal accept). The Phase-2 impl follow-up decomposed each spec-side skill into prose + CLI. The mapped test asserts all seven spec-side CLI names (seed, propose-change, revise, critique, doctor, prune-history, next) pre-populate with core's argv-form reference defaults; the sibling test_validate_livespec_config_spec_clis_individually_overridable asserts overriding one CLI leaves every sibling at its default — covering per-CLI config naming, reference-default pre-population, and individual overridability.

heading: ## CLI shape conventions
reason: Landed in v103 (contract-and-reference-implementations-phase-1 proposal accept). Real test ID populated in the Phase-2 impl follow-up that asserts the one-binary-per-side subcommand shape, --json stable schemas, stdin/stdout payload handling, exit-code-table reuse, and explicit project-root addressing across the named CLIs.

heading: ## Error path 3 — version-contiguity gap in history
reason: scenarios.md tier rule (epic li-scetier, dev-tooling v0.9.0): scenario entries MUST point at an integration-tier test (tests.e2e / tests.prompts / tests.integration / tests.consumer / tests.bin), not a unit test. The prior pointer tests.livespec.doctor.static.test_version_contiguity is unit-tier and exercises the version-contiguity predicate in isolation, not the end-to-end /livespec:doctor invocation against a real v001/v003-no-v002 history gap. No existing e2e/integration test seeds a history gap and asserts the version-contiguity fail finding: tests.e2e.test_doctor_fail_then_fix exercises doctor static-fail-then-fix but trips bcp14-keyword-wellformedness, not version-contiguity. Honest TODO bridge pending a new e2e doctor-version-contiguity test.

heading: ## Reference discipline
reason: Added by the revise pass landing the propose-change at proposed_changes/reference-discipline-invariant.md. Codifies two paired invariants (SPECIFICATION/ tree cannot reference outside content except via an external_references allowlist in .livespec.jsonc; source code cannot cite spec sections via §"…"). Real test ID populated in the impl follow-up work-item that authors livespec/doctor/static/no_cross_spec_reference.py + no_spec_section_citation_in_code.py plus paired tests and retroactive sweep of existing violations.

heading: ## Doctor per-finding disposition dialogue
reason: Added by the revise pass landing the propose-change at proposed_changes/doctor-uniform-per-finding-disposition.md. Codifies that doctor's per-finding disposition dialogue MUST apply uniformly to every non-pass finding regardless of phase, with a five-option menu (fix-now, capture-as-work-item, propose-change, defer, dismiss). Real test ID populated in the impl follow-up that regenerates .claude-plugin/skills/doctor/SKILL.md to expand Step 11 from three options to five and wire the dialogue into the Exit 3 static-fail path.

heading: ## CLI end-to-end harness contract
reason: Landed in v097 (cli-end-to-end-harness-contract proposal accept, epic li-e2ecli Phase 1). The contract mandates a top-of-pyramid CLI-driven user-surface e2e tier. Real test ID populated by Epic D Waves 11-12: the harness ships from livespec-dev-tooling (li-e2ecdt) and each consumer repo wires tests/e2e-cli/ fixtures (li-e2eclv livespec, li-e2ecpx impl-plaintext).

heading: ## Fleet agent-instruction core
reason: Landed by the revise pass accepting the proposed change that introduced the fleet-universal agent-instruction core. The heading mandates several paired obligations; the mapped test covers the load-bearing beads-access-guard MUST ("Beads-backed members MUST ship a beads-access guard: a Claude Code PreToolUse hook ... that blocks a bare bd, dolt, or direct tenant mysql invocation unless it runs under a recognized per-project credential-injection wrapper"). tests.test_template_beads_access_guard exercises the template-shipped guard's should_block predicate and its main stdin->decision deny/allow path against bare vs. wrapped bd/dolt/tenant-mysql commands. The remaining facets — the fleet-universal core's content (mutation protocol, daily commands, beads-runtime prereqs), the AGENTS.md / .claude/CLAUDE.md symlink shape, and the fleet-wide OBLIGATION_ROWS instruction-surface enforcement — ship from livespec-dev-tooling's fleet-membership obligation suite with its own tests; no live drift meanwhile (this repo's guard + symlink shape are present and green).

heading: ## Built-in template contracts
reason: Relocated into the main contracts.md from the retired dogfooding sub-specs (SPECIFICATION/templates/livespec and SPECIFICATION/templates/minimal) in v135. Consolidates several template-internal contracts; the mapped test covers the load-bearing minimal-template MUST ("The doctor static gherkin-blank-line-format check MUST exempt minimal-shape spec_roots whose SPECIFICATION.md contains no fenced ```gherkin blocks (emitting status='skipped')"): it seeds a minimal-shape single-file SPECIFICATION.md with no gherkin fences and asserts status='skipped' with the exemption message, and its sibling test_run_applies_normally_for_minimal_shape_with_gherkin_blocks asserts the check applies normally when gherkin IS present. The other facets — the livespec template's NLSpec reference doc and doctor_llm_*_checks_prompt paths, and the minimal template's spec_root './' single-file shape, null doctor_llm opt-out, and HTML-comment region markers — are exercised by the template-config (tests.livespec.validate.test_template_config) and doctor-static suites; no live drift meanwhile.
